title: malus × purpurea f. eleyi
intro: Malus × purpurea f. eleyi, also known as Eley's crabapple, is a cultivar of the crabapple tree (Malus × purpurea) known for its unique characteristics and medicinal properties. The plant is used in herbal preparations to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Eley's crabapple include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. It can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, or used in combination with other herbs to create remedies for respiratory and digestive issues.
